subject: Cloud Computing
title: From Physical to Virtual Data Centers
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on From Physical to Virtual Data Centers.

hook.text: Companies used to buy servers, wait weeks for delivery, then spend days setting them up.

buildup.text: Virtual data centers changed this. Spin up a hundred servers in minutes, delete them when done.

discovery.text: Infrastructure became code. Entire data centers defined in a text file, deployed instantly.

twist.text: This terrified IT teams. Their job had been managing physical hardware for decades.

climax.text: The shift from physical to virtual wasn't just technical. It changed who controlled computing.

punchline.text: Hardware became software. And software moves fast.

quiz.question: What does 'infrastructure as code' mean?
options: Defining server infrastructure in text files that can be deployed automatically
Writing code that runs only on physical servers
Replacing all software engineers with hardware engineers
